raw_output_payload.reasoning:
We need a bash command to delete files matching patterns under /system/folder3/temp. Use find with -type f -name patterns. Patterns: .DS_Store, '._.DS_Store', '._*', '.TemporaryItems', '.apdisk'. Use -delete. Also directories? It says files and directories under that tree that match with one of the name patterns. Some patterns may be directories? .TemporaryItems is a directory. .apdisk maybe file? Use -depth to delete directories after contents. Command:

find /system/folder3/temp -depth \( -name '.DS_Store' -o -name '._.DS_Store' -o -name '._*' -o -name '.TemporaryItems' -o -name '.apdisk' \) -exec rm -rf {} +

But better use -delete. For directories need -type d? But -delete works for both. Use -depth to delete directories after contents. So:

find /system/folder3/temp -depth \( -name '.DS_Store' -o -name '._.DS_Store' -o -name '._*' -o -name '.TemporaryItems' -o -name '.apdisk' \) -delete

Return that.
